title: Remove File upload tab; make File dockable in board mode
prompt:
Two changes to the File surface in web-ui:

1) Remove the file upload tab entirely — it is no longer needed. The File filesystem view currently has two sub-tabs (文件系统 / 上传). Remove the 上传 (upload) sub-tab and its UI. Since only the 文件系统 view remains, collapse away the now-single-tab switcher so there is no leftover tab chrome. Keep the OS drag-in upload path only if it exists independently of the removed tab; otherwise remove the upload code paths that are now dead (e.g. the workspaceFs.uploadFile-driven tab UI). Do not break the read/edit/create/rename/move/delete filesystem explorer functionality.

2) In board mode, File should be positioned like the Kanban agent sidebar: anchored in the top-right corner and dockable to either the left or right side. Mirror the existing dockable-home-chat-panel behavior (left/right docking) for the File surface so users can toggle its side. Reuse the existing dock/anchor pattern rather than inventing a new one.

Context pointers: the File surface lives under web-ui/src/components/file-surface/ and the filesystem explorer / upload code (workspaceFs tRPC, FileTree, upload dialog). The dockable panel reference is the home chat dockable panel (left/right/float, react-rnd for float). Match existing design tokens and UI primitives per AGENTS.md.

Verify web:typecheck passes and the File surface still opens, reads, and edits files after the changes.
